summary: This source is an academic paper analyzing the socio-political, familial, and geographic factors that allowed Hypatia of Alexandria to succeed as a female scholar in antiquity. Because the text relies on posthumous historical accounts and focuses primarily on her societal context rather than her original writings, it contains very little of Hypatia's own direct teachings or frameworks.

principles.statement: True love and devotion should be directed toward the immaterial 'Beautiful', not the physical human body.
principles.rationale: Rooted in Neoplatonic thought, physical passion is viewed as a base distraction from higher intellectual and spiritual pursuits.
principles.source_quote: This is what you are in love with, young man, and not with the Beautiful

signature_quotes.text: This is what you are in love with, young man, and not with the Beautiful
signature_quotes.context: Reportedly said to a student who had fallen in love with her, while throwing a menstrual rag at him to demonstrate the unclean nature of the physical body and cure him of his passion.

anti_patterns.description: Succumbing to physical passion and lust for the human body.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It distracts the mind from the pursuit of higher culture and the immaterial 'Beautiful', focusing instead on the base or 'unclean' nature of the material world.
